reasoning: PoC mimics the production BSC-style predeploy using vm.etch at SystemAddresses.GOVERNANCE without copying slot 0/1 — the exact omission the static auditor flagged. On main (a623eab), forge test PASSES with all four assertion clusters holding: (1) Governance.owner()==address(0) while a normally-constructed twin has owner()==intendedOwner (sanity check proves bytecode is fine, flaw is purely the missing storage seed); (2) addExecutor reverts for both an arbitrary caller and the *intended* owner (slot 0 was never seeded so even the 'right' address has no authority); (3) all three recovery paths (transferOwnership, acceptOwnership, renounceOwnership) revert; (4) execute(...) reverts on the onlyExecutor gate because _executors is permanently empty. This directly confirms the static EXISTS verdict's impact claim (on-chain governance execution permanently bricked). The only residual concern is the counterargument the static report itself raises: an out-of-repo gravity-reth node could in principle apply a hardcoded slot-0 state override at chain start; this PoC cannot disprove that since the node source is not in this repo, but the static auditor's reasoning (no config field, no hook, and the unit-test harness's explicit vm.store workaround all suggest the node does not do this) remains persuasive. Dynamic confidence: HIGH on the Solidity/genesis-tool layer.
